commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: If God invariably listened to every supplicant equally, he might appear to us to act from some necessity rather than from his own free will.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not right to take the children's bread, and to cast it unto the dogs. Those who possess the Holy Spirit search out “the deep places of God”—in other words, they attain the hidden secrets that surround prophecy. But it is forbidden to share holy things with dogs, as long as they remain wild. It is never appropriate to dilute the pure stream of divinity, the living water, for interests that are full of malice, disturbed persons, still without faith, who are unrestrained in barking at the hunt.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: From his children, and hand it to dogs? "

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
